name: Deduplicate a Notion database by keeping the newest row and archiving the rest

parameters.content:
## Deduplicate a Notion database by keeping the newest row and archiving the rest

### How it works

1. On a daily schedule, it reads every row from the Notion database you choose.
2. A Code node groups the rows by a property you set and finds any group with more than one row.
3. For each group it keeps one row (the newest by default) and marks the others as duplicates.
4. Each duplicate is archived to the Notion trash, where it stays recoverable for 30 days.
5. A one-line recap is appended to a log page on every run, including runs that find no duplicates.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Connect a Notion credential and share the target database and the log page with the integration.
- [ ] In Get Database Rows, select the database you want to deduplicate.
- [ ] In Find Duplicates and Build Recap, set MATCH_PROPERTY and KEEP_RULE at the top of the code.
- [ ] In Append Run to Audit Log, paste the URL of the page that should receive the recap.

### Customization

Set KEEP_RULE to newest, oldest, or mostFilled to decide which row survives, and adjust Every Day at 2am to run on any schedule you prefer.

## Fetch database rows

Runs on a daily schedule and pulls every row with full property data.

## Find and route duplicates

Groups rows by your chosen property, picks a keeper per group, and routes the duplicates onward.

## Archive duplicates and log the run

Archives each duplicate to the Notion trash and appends a recap line to your log page.
